name: Dance
description: Ballet, tap, salsa, hip-hop — across cultures and centuries.

questions.q: Which king of France is widely credited as the founder of classical ballet?

source.notes: He danced ballet himself and founded the Académie Royale de Danse in 1661.

questions.q: In what year was the world's first ballet academy, the Académie Royale de Danse, founded?

source.notes: Founded in Paris by Louis XIV.

questions.q: What time signature is used for the waltz?
Which country's Carnival celebrations are most associated with the samba?
Which classical Indian dance form is named for its origins in Tamil Nadu?

source.notes: One of eight classical Indian dance forms recognized by the Sangeet Natak Akademi.

questions.q: From which region of India does the upbeat folk dance Bhangra originate?

source.notes: Original answer mis-identified Bhangra as 'South American' — it is from Punjab, India / Pakistan.

questions.q: Which Tchaikovsky ballet tells the story of Princess Odette, who is cursed to live as a swan?

source.notes: Original answer was incorrect: Swan Lake (1877) tells the swan story; The Nutcracker (1892) is a different Tchaikovsky ballet.

questions.q: Which type of dance is performed in shoes with metal plates on the toe and heel?
Which legendary American dancer, nicknamed 'Bojangles', helped popularize tap dance in early 20th-century film?
The 'Dancing Plague of 1518' famously broke out in which European city?

source.original_a: Mass hysteria caused by uncontrollable dancing
source.notes: An outbreak of involuntary dancing in Strasbourg (then in the Holy Roman Empire) lasting weeks.

questions.q: Michael Jackson's 1983 music video popularized which dance style with its iconic group choreography?
Hip-hop dance evolved primarily from which cultural context?
choices: African-American and Latino youth culture in 1970s NYC

source.notes: Born in the Bronx alongside hip-hop music; styles include breaking, locking, popping.

questions.q: Which traditional Irish dance is typically set to fast 6/8 music?
What is the term for a couples dance with a partner-swap, prompts called by a 'caller', commonly seen at country gatherings?

source.notes: The official folk dance of more than 20 U.S. states.

questions.q: From which European region did the polka dance originate in the 19th century?

source.notes: Originated in Bohemia (modern-day Czech Republic) around 1830.

questions.q: Which Latin dance, originating in Argentina, is known for its dramatic close-hold and the 'slow-slow-quick-quick-slow' rhythm?

source.notes: Originated in Buenos Aires and Montevideo in the late 1800s.

questions.q: Which Middle Eastern dance is characterized by isolated hip and torso movements?

source.notes: Also known as 'raqs sharqi' (Arabic for 'eastern dance').

questions.q: Which 20th-century dance form, pioneered by Martha Graham, broke from classical ballet's strict technique?

source.notes: Graham's company is the oldest American dance company (founded 1926).

questions.q: On the toes — what is the ballet technique of dancing on the very tips of the toes called?
Which is the oldest known archaeological site to depict dance, with rock paintings roughly 10,000 years old?

source.notes: Bhimbetka rock shelters in central India; UNESCO World Heritage Site.
